title: Job Openings at Staff-Recruit
content:
Staff-Recruit is a leading Human Resource and Management Consulting firm that provides a range of recruitment, selection, outsourcing, training & development solutions. We are a dedicated team of professional consultants offering top-of-the-line executive recruitment and selection services. We cater to the needs of a range of professionals seeking employment and work together to create effective solutions using our networks and strong client base.

STAFF-RECRUIT was founded in 2020 in Lagos, Nigeria. We have served clients across the public and private sectors and various industries, including Banking, Oil and Gas, Insurance, Information Technology, Real Estate, Energy, FMCG, Manufacturing, and many more.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Inventory Lead

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Inventory Lead is responsible for overseeing all inventory control activities to ensure the accuracy, availability, and integrity of stock records.
	The role provides leadership to the Inventory Officer, ensures compliance with inventory management procedures, maintains optimal stock levels, and supports efficient procurement and production operations through effective inventory planning and reporting.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor inventory movements to ensure all receipts, issues, transfers, and adjustments are accurately captured in the inventory management system.
	Conduct regular verification of inventory records and ensure discrepancies are investigated and resolved promptly.
	Ensure monthly reconciliation of physical inventory, bin cards (both hard copy and electronic records), and system balances to maintain accurate stock records.
	Ensure all Material Requisition Forms (MRFs), Goods Received Notes (GRNs), and other inventory documents are properly completed, verified, and submitted within the required timelines.
	Plan, coordinate, and supervise periodic stock counts and annual stock audits to ensure physical inventory aligns with recorded balances.
	Review and validate inventory adjustments with appropriate approvals and supporting documentation.
	Monitor inventory levels and ensure timely replenishment by initiating restock requests before critical stock levels are reached.
	Maintain optimum inventory levels to minimize stock shortages, excess inventory, and obsolete materials.
	Ensure proper storage, handling, labeling, and preservation of raw materials, consumables, spare parts, and finished goods in line with company standards.
	Prepare inventory performance reports and provide management with recommendations for improving stock accuracy and inventory efficiency.
	Maintain complete and organized inventory documentation for internal and external audits.
	Liaise with the Procurement team to ensure timely sourcing and replenishment of inventory items.
	Provide inventory status updates to support production planning and operational activities.
	Ensure critical materials are always available to prevent operational disruptions.


Qualifications & Experience
Education:


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, Accounting, or a related field.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in inventory, warehouse, or supply chain management, with at least 1 year in a supervisory role.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory/ERP systems.


Skills:


	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and leadership abilities.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 23rd July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Proximity to VGC, Ajah, Lagos State, is required for this position.

 

 

2.) Operation Officer 

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos 

Employment Type: Full-time
Work type: Fully Onsite

Job Summary


	The Operations Officer is responsible for assisting in coordinating warehouse and delivery operations to ensure the timely receipt, storage, quality inspection, and distribution of materials.
	The role ensures accurate documentation, efficient inventory handling, and excellent customer service while supporting smooth day-to-day operational activities.


Job Description


	Receiving and checking deliveries to ensure that the correct amount and type of stock is delivered
	Carry out packing, crating, and warehousing, and storage duties in preparation for delivery activities.
	Provide excellent customer service and maintain relationships with client rep.
	Regularly Update logs and order forms
	Signing delivery notes upon receipt of shipments.
	Heeding notes about appropriate procedures for handling stock, particularly when fragile.
	Unpacking items and arranging these on shelves, if needed.
	Preparing our clients' orders by gathering and loading stock.
	Ensuring that the vehicle remains clean, sanitary, and well-organized for delivery at all times.
	Ensure materials/items are appropriately stored and packed for delivery.
	Handles the quality check of materials/items prior to delivery.
	Provide a list of all equipment moved from the consolidation areas into sponsor shipping channels to project coordinators.
	Promptly answer the questions of staff and other stakeholders
	Prepare and file forms and other documents.
	Analyze all delivery activities and forward suggestions for improvement to the Warehouse Logistic Officer.


Requirements / Skill / Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Logistics, Business Administration, or Transportation, Industrial Distribution, Industrial Engineering, or Supply Chain Management, or in a related field.
	Work experience in warehouse, logistic or supply chain management.
	Good organizational and communication skills.
	Willingness and ability to adapt to changing work requirements and priorities that may require overtime or extended hours.
	Proximity to Lekki/Ajah is a plus.


Remuneration
N100,000 – 150,000 monthly depending on qualifications and experience.

Application Closing Date: 25th July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using “OPERATION OFFICER” as the subject of the mail.

Note: Proximity to Lekki/Ajah is a plus.

 

 

3.) Technical Sales Officer 

Job Location: Ajah

Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Technical Sales Officer is responsible for identifying tender opportunities, preparing competitive bid submissions, coordinating technical and commercial documentation, and ensuring compliance with client requirements to support business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor and identify relevant bid and tender opportunities from public and private sectors.
	Review tender documents to determine requirements, eligibility, and submission timelines.
	Coordinate the preparation and submission of technical and commercial bid documents.
	Prepare proposals, quotations, and tender responses in line with client specifications.
	Liaise with internal departments to obtain technical information, pricing, certifications, and supporting documents.
	Ensure all bid submissions are complete, accurate, and submitted within deadlines.
	Maintain an up-to-date database of tender opportunities, bid submissions, and outcomes.
	Follow up on submitted bids and maintain communication with clients throughout the tender process.
	Prepare post-bid reports and recommend improvements to increase bid success rates.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, procurement regulations, and client requirements.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Number of bids/tenders submitted.
	Bid submission accuracy and compliance rate.
	Tender win rate.
	Timeliness of bid submissions.
	Revenue generated from successful bids.
	Client satisfaction and responsiveness.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's degree or HND in Engineering, Business Administration, Marketing, Procurement, or a related field.
	1–3 years of experience in bid management, tender administration, technical sales, or business development.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
	Proximity to VGC, Ajah, Lagos State, is required for this position.


Salary
N180,000 - N220,000 per month. depending on qualifications and experience.

Application Closing Date: 24th July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using “TECHNICAL SALES OFFICER” as the subject of the mail.

Note: Proximity to VGC, Ajah, Lagos State, is required for this position.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Audit Officer Job at Brit Properties Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Brit Properties Nigeria Limited is a property development company incorporated in Nigeria by Companies and Allied Matters Act. Our primary business interests are land, infrastructure/property development, land survey, property marketing and estate agency. We strive to provide exceptional customer service while offering our clients the best property options. We are committed to making good property decision for both existing and potential clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Audit Officer

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	Plan and execute internal audits across various departments and business units.
	Review financial records, operational processes, and internal control systems.
	Assess compliance with company policies, procedures, and applicable regulations.
	Identify control weaknesses, risks, and areas for improvement.
	Prepare clear and accurate audit reports highlighting findings and recommendations.
	Follow up on audit findings and monitor implementation of corrective actions.
	Verify the accuracy and completeness of financial and operational information.
	Conduct inventory, cash, and asset audits as required.
	Maintain proper documentation of audit work, evidence, and working papers.
	Support risk assessments and development of audit plans.
	Assist external auditors by providing relevant information and documentation.
	Promote best practices and continuous improvement within the organization.


Requirements
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3–5 years of experience in internal audit, external audit, accounting, or compliance roles.
	Professional certification such as Institute of Internal Auditors (CIA), ACCA, ACA, or equivalent is an advantage.


Required Skills and Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of auditing standards and internal controls.
	Good understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Ability to prepare professional audit reports.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and audit/accounting software.


Benefits


	Salary: N120,000 - N150,000 per month.
	Employees who complete one (1) year of service will receive a 50% discount on the purchase of any plot of land of their choice.
	Employees who complete two (2) years of service will receive one frea plot of land.
	Comprehensive Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) coverage.
	Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.
	Career growth in a dynamic and innovative organization.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Channel Accounting Executive",
  "description": "Airtel Nigeria (Airtel Networks Limited) is a leading telecommunications services provider in Nigeria headquartered in Lagos, the commercial nerve-centre of Nigeria. The telco ranks amongst the top four mobile service providers in terms of subscribers with a customer base of more than 39.8 million. The company&#39;s product offerings include 2G, 3G and 4G wireless services, mobile commerce and enterprise services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Channel Accounting Executive

Job Identification: 808
Job Location: Lagos

About the Role


	The job purpose is to ensure that the Finance function of supporting the Sales Ordering Process (SOP) is carried out efficiently and effectively.&nbsp;


Responsibilities
Sales Ordering support:


	Ensure that all Channel Partners&rsquo; orders awaiting Finance&rsquo;s override are processed promptly by following up with ACE Billing Team.
	Ensure no bad debt to Airtel arising from credit utilization.
	Ensure no debit balance for Cash Channel Partners.


Daily Reporting:


	Ensure daily Channel Partners&rsquo; overdue report tracking.
	Monitor daily credit limits utilization.
	Daily tracking of the CPs BG expiration / validity.
	Daily ERC shop Vs Pretups Reconciliation.


Book-Keeping and Hygiene:


	Ensure that Channel Partners&rsquo; statements of accounts are reconciled daily to militate against risk to Airtel.
	Daily Tracking of Stock Transfer/Return for all Airtel Stock are process within agreed control.


Finance Month-end activities &amp; Ad-hoc functions:


	Involved in Finance&rsquo;s month end book closure activities.&nbsp;
	Carry out other ad-hoc functions that may be assigned.


Educational Qualifications&nbsp;&nbsp;


	University Degree in Accounting or related disciplines
	ACA is an added advantage.


Relevant Experience &amp; Functional / Technical Skills:


	3-5 years of relevant experience
	Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office tools
	Adept user of accounting packages; Sage Line 500 and Oracle ERP
	Good Communication (Oral &amp; Written) and Inter-personal skills
	Good numerate and Analytical skills
	Spontaneous and logical.

Channel Accounting Executive Job at Airtel Nigeria




        
    

        
        Airtel Nigeria (Airtel Networks Limited) is a leading telecommunications services provider in Nigeria headquartered in Lagos, the commercial nerve-centre of Nigeria. The telco ranks amongst the top four mobile service providers in terms of subscribers with a customer base of more than 39.8 million. The company's product offerings include 2G, 3G and 4G wireless services, mobile commerce and enterprise services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Channel Accounting Executive

Job Identification: 808
Job Location: Lagos

About the Role
     

            
    




	The job purpose is to ensure that the Finance function of supporting the Sales Ordering Process (SOP) is carried out efficiently and effectively. 


Responsibilities
Sales Ordering support:


	Ensure that all Channel Partners’ orders awaiting Finance’s override are processed promptly by following up with ACE Billing Team.
	Ensure no bad debt to Airtel arising from credit utilization.
	Ensure no debit balance for Cash Channel Partners.


Daily Reporting:


	Ensure daily Channel Partners’ overdue report tracking.
	Monitor daily credit limits utilization.
	Daily tracking of the CPs BG expiration / validity.
	Daily ERC shop Vs Pretups Reconciliation.


Book-Keeping and Hygiene:


	Ensure that Channel Partners’ statements of accounts are reconciled daily to militate against risk to Airtel.
	Daily Tracking of Stock Transfer/Return for all Airtel Stock are process within agreed control.


Finance Month-end activities & Ad-hoc functions:


	Involved in Finance’s month end book closure activities. 
	Carry out other ad-hoc functions that may be assigned.


Educational Qualifications  


	University Degree in Accounting or related disciplines
	ACA is an added advantage.


Relevant Experience & Functional / Technical Skills:


	3-5 years of relevant experience
	Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office tools
	Adept user of accounting packages; Sage Line 500 and Oracle ERP
	Good Communication (Oral & Written) and Inter-personal skills
	Good numerate and Analytical skills
	Spontaneous and logical.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Offline Customer Support Officer",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Offline Customer Support Officer

Job Location: Ibadan - Bodija or Challenge Road, Oyo
Department: Customer Support Operations
Reports to: Regional Team Lead

About this role


	The Customer Support Officer provides high-quality, timely, and professional support to walk-in customers nationwide and customers in WhatsApp Groups. The role focuses on resolving customer enquiries, technical issues, and service-related concerns while delivering a consistent customer experience aligned with the Bank&rsquo;s standards and values.
	The officer serves as the first point of contact, collaborating with internal teams to ensure prompt issue resolution and offering insights to improve customer experience based on logged issues and customer feedback.


Responsibilities


	Serve as the first point of contact for walk-in customers nationwide and respond to customer inquiries on WhatsApp platforms.
	Provide prompt, accurate, and high-quality responses to customer inquiries and service requests.
	Log all customer support requests accurately for tracking and follow-up.
	Coordinate with relevant internal teams to ensure timely resolution of customer issues.
	Escalate complex or unresolved issues to the Regional/State Team Lead as required.
	Adhere to approved communication scripts, service guidelines, and operational procedures.
	Collect and document customer feedback on products and services to support continuous improvement.
	Identify customer needs and offer appropriate solutions to ensure satisfaction.
	Build and maintain strong, trust-based relationships with customers through clear and effective communication.
	Maintain confidentiality and data privacy of customer information at all times.


Experience &amp; Background


	1&ndash;2 years of relevant customer service or support experience.
	Minimum educational qualification of BSc or HND.
	Must have completed NYSC or possess a valid exemption/exclusion certificate.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent written and verbal communication skills (specifically for handling walk-ins and managing WhatsApp group communications).
	Strong problem-solving and issue-resolution abilities.
	Customer-centric mindset with high empathy and emotional intelligence.
	Ability to collaborate effectively with internal cross-functional teams.
	Meticulous attention to detail for tracking, documentation, and data logging.
	Strict adherence to operational procedures, data privacy, and confidentiality guidelines.


What success looks like in this role


	High customer satisfaction scores and positive feedback from walk-in and WhatsApp group interactions.
	Consistently meeting or exceeding response and resolution timelines (SLAs) for logged inquiries.
	Accurate and thorough logging of all customer interactions to prevent unresolved gaps.
	Strong operational discipline with minimal escalations due to unresolved baseline issues.
	Proactive collection of customer feedback that leads to actionable internal service improvements.


Why join us


	Competitive compensation, benefits, and career growth opportunities.
	Support for professional development.

Offline Customer Support Officer Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Offline Customer Support Officer

Job Location: Ibadan - Bodija or Challenge Road, Oyo
Department: Customer Support Operations
Reports to: Regional Team Lead

About this role
     

            
    




	The Customer Support Officer provides high-quality, timely, and professional support to walk-in customers nationwide and customers in WhatsApp Groups. The role focuses on resolving customer enquiries, technical issues, and service-related concerns while delivering a consistent customer experience aligned with the Bank’s standards and values.
	The officer serves as the first point of contact, collaborating with internal teams to ensure prompt issue resolution and offering insights to improve customer experience based on logged issues and customer feedback.


Responsibilities


	Serve as the first point of contact for walk-in customers nationwide and respond to customer inquiries on WhatsApp platforms.
	Provide prompt, accurate, and high-quality responses to customer inquiries and service requests.
	Log all customer support requests accurately for tracking and follow-up.
	Coordinate with relevant internal teams to ensure timely resolution of customer issues.
	Escalate complex or unresolved issues to the Regional/State Team Lead as required.
	Adhere to approved communication scripts, service guidelines, and operational procedures.
	Collect and document customer feedback on products and services to support continuous improvement.
	Identify customer needs and offer appropriate solutions to ensure satisfaction.
	Build and maintain strong, trust-based relationships with customers through clear and effective communication.
	Maintain confidentiality and data privacy of customer information at all times.


Experience & Background


	1–2 years of relevant customer service or support experience.
	Minimum educational qualification of BSc or HND.
	Must have completed NYSC or possess a valid exemption/exclusion certificate.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent written and verbal communication skills (specifically for handling walk-ins and managing WhatsApp group communications).
	Strong problem-solving and issue-resolution abilities.
	Customer-centric mindset with high empathy and emotional intelligence.
	Ability to collaborate effectively with internal cross-functional teams.
	Meticulous attention to detail for tracking, documentation, and data logging.
	Strict adherence to operational procedures, data privacy, and confidentiality guidelines.


What success looks like in this role


	High customer satisfaction scores and positive feedback from walk-in and WhatsApp group interactions.
	Consistently meeting or exceeding response and resolution timelines (SLAs) for logged inquiries.
	Accurate and thorough logging of all customer interactions to prevent unresolved gaps.
	Strong operational discipline with minimal escalations due to unresolved baseline issues.
	Proactive collection of customer feedback that leads to actionable internal service improvements.


Why join us


	Competitive compensation, benefits, and career growth opportunities.
	Support for professional development.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Assurance Lead (Medical Doctor)",
  "description": "Redeemer&rsquo;s Health Village is committed to delivering excellent, patient-centered healthcare in a compassionate environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Lead (Medical Doctor)

Job Location: Redeemer&rsquo;s Health Village (RHV), Redemption City, Ogun&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Redeemer&rsquo;s Health Village (RHV) is seeking a highly analytical, detail-oriented, and quality-driven Medical Doctor to join our team as Quality Assurance Lead.
	This role is responsible for driving quality improvement initiatives across the hospital, ensuring compliance with clinical and operational standards, identifying quality gaps, and promoting a culture of continuous improvement that enhances patient safety and clinical outcomes.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead the implementation and continuous improvement of the hospital&rsquo;s Quality Management System.
	Monitor compliance with clinical protocols, policies, regulatory requirements, and accreditation standards.
	Conduct regular quality audits, inspections, and process reviews across all departments.
	Identify, investigate, and escalate quality, patient safety, and compliance issues promptly.
	Perform root cause analyses and coordinate corrective and preventive actions.
	Review clinical documentation and medical records for quality and compliance.
	Monitor quality indicators, analyse trends, and prepare reports for leadership.
	Support incident reporting, investigations, and quality improvement projects.
	Collaborate with clinical and non-clinical teams to improve service delivery and patient outcomes.
	Facilitate quality improvement training and promote a culture of accountability and excellence.


Requirements


	Medical Degree (MBBS or equivalent) with a current licence to practise.
	Minimum of 3 years of post-qualification experience, with experience in hospital quality assurance, clinical governance, patient safety, or healthcare quality management being an advantage.
	Strong understanding of healthcare quality standards, clinical governance, infection prevention, and patient safety principles.
	Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to identify risks, inconsistencies, and opportunities for improvement.
	Ability to objectively identify and call out quality issues regardless of department or seniority.
	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent report writing, communication, and presentation skills.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and sound judgment.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and quality reporting tools.


What We Are Looking For:
The ideal candidate is someone who:


	Has an uncompromising commitment to quality and patient safety.
	Notices what others miss.
	Is confident in raising quality concerns professionally and objectively.
	Uses data to drive decisions and continuous improvement.
	Can influence multidisciplinary teams while maintaining excellent working relationships.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified should forward their application, detailed CV, and supporting documents to: careers@redeemershealthvillage.org using the Job Position as the subject of the mail",

Quality Assurance Lead (Medical Doctor) Job at Redeemer’s Health Village




        
    

        
        Redeemer’s Health Village is committed to delivering excellent, patient-centered healthcare in a compassionate environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Lead (Medical Doctor)

Job Location: Redeemer’s Health Village (RHV), Redemption City, Ogun 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	Redeemer’s Health Village (RHV) is seeking a highly analytical, detail-oriented, and quality-driven Medical Doctor to join our team as Quality Assurance Lead.
	This role is responsible for driving quality improvement initiatives across the hospital, ensuring compliance with clinical and operational standards, identifying quality gaps, and promoting a culture of continuous improvement that enhances patient safety and clinical outcomes.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead the implementation and continuous improvement of the hospital’s Quality Management System.
	Monitor compliance with clinical protocols, policies, regulatory requirements, and accreditation standards.
	Conduct regular quality audits, inspections, and process reviews across all departments.
	Identify, investigate, and escalate quality, patient safety, and compliance issues promptly.
	Perform root cause analyses and coordinate corrective and preventive actions.
	Review clinical documentation and medical records for quality and compliance.
	Monitor quality indicators, analyse trends, and prepare reports for leadership.
	Support incident reporting, investigations, and quality improvement projects.
	Collaborate with clinical and non-clinical teams to improve service delivery and patient outcomes.
	Facilitate quality improvement training and promote a culture of accountability and excellence.


Requirements


	Medical Degree (MBBS or equivalent) with a current licence to practise.
	Minimum of 3 years of post-qualification experience, with experience in hospital quality assurance, clinical governance, patient safety, or healthcare quality management being an advantage.
	Strong understanding of healthcare quality standards, clinical governance, infection prevention, and patient safety principles.
	Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to identify risks, inconsistencies, and opportunities for improvement.
	Ability to objectively identify and call out quality issues regardless of department or seniority.
	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent report writing, communication, and presentation skills.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and sound judgment.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and quality reporting tools.


What We Are Looking For:
The ideal candidate is someone who:


	Has an uncompromising commitment to quality and patient safety.
	Notices what others miss.
	Is confident in raising quality concerns professionally and objectively.
	Uses data to drive decisions and continuous improvement.
